name: io.github.localgpt-app/localgpt-gen
title: LocalGPT Gen
description: AI-driven 3D world builder: Bevy visuals, procedural audio, and entity behaviors.

runtimeArguments.name: --rm
runtimeArguments.description: Remove container after the session exits

runtimeArguments.name: -i
runtimeArguments.description: Keep stdin open (required for stdio transport)

runtimeArguments.name: --network
runtimeArguments.description: Network mode. Use 'host' if local models (Ollama, llama.cpp) are running on the host.

runtimeArguments.name: -v
runtimeArguments.description: Mount a local workspace directory. Exposes memory, skills, and saved worlds to the container.

packageArguments.name: subcommand
packageArguments.description: Start in MCP server mode with stdio transport

environmentVariables.name: LOCALGPT_WORKSPACE
environmentVariables.description: Path to workspace directory (memory, skills, saved worlds). Defaults to ~/.local/share/localgpt/workspace.

environmentVariables.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
environmentVariables.description: Anthropic API key for direct claude-* model access. Not required if using claude-cli provider.

environmentVariables.name: OPENAI_API_KEY
environmentVariables.description: OpenAI API key for GPT-4o or OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenRouter, Groq, DeepSeek, etc.).

visual.description: Real-time 3D scene rendering via Bevy engine. Opens an interactive viewport for scene exploration with first-person/third-person navigation, orbit camera, and a live inspector overlay.

audio.description: Procedural environmental soundscapes and spatial audio emitters synthesized in real time. No pre-recorded samples — all audio is generated algorithmically.
features: Distance-attenuated spatial emitters attached to scene entities
Web Audio API export for standalone HTML worlds

behavior.description: Data-driven entity behaviors, interactive logic, physics, NPCs, and trigger/action wiring — no scripting required.
features: Composable entity animations: orbit, spin, bob, look_at, pulse, path_follow, bounce
NPC characters with idle, patrol, and wander AI
Trigger/action system: proximity, click, area, collision, timer
Entity event wiring (source event → target action)
Physics bodies and constraints (dynamic, static, kinematic)
